Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Pane Content Storage — Best Approach

## Context

The current pane snapshot approach has three problems:

1. **Opaque filenames** — files named `3.txt`, `7.txt` by numeric pane ID, which resets on server restart and is unreadable
2. **No stale cleanup** — files for closed panes accumulate forever; old IDs from previous server sessions leave ghost files
3. **Shallow scrollback** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` is bounded by `history-limit`, which defaults to only 2 000 lines

The fix is straightforward: readable `session--window--pane.txt` naming, atomic stale-file cleanup each cycle, and a large `history-limit` so the scrollback is deep enough to be genuinely useful.

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

---

## Changes

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`

Add after the existing session/window options:

```
set -g history-limit 50000
```

---

### 2. Standalone script + Nix reference

**Create:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done
```

**Update:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` — replace the inline `text = ''...''` block with a file source:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  source = ./session-logger.sh;
};
```

Key changes vs current:
- **Standalone file** — no Nix string escaping, easier to edit/test directly
- **Readable names** — `work--0--0.txt` instead of `3.txt`
- **Stale cleanup** — rotate-to-`.old`, recreate live, delete `.old`

---

### 3. Update `_tsh_function` to parse filename

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

Replace the metadata-header parsing with filename parsing:

```fish
# work--0--0.txt → sess=work, widx=0
set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace '.txt' '')
set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
set -l sess $parts[1]
set -l widx $parts[2]
```

Remove the `head -n 1` / `string match -rg 'session=...'` lines.

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `set -g history-limit 50000` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh` | **create** — standalone script |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Replace inline `text=` with `source = ./session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | Parse session/window from filename, drop header parsing |

---

## Verification

1. After server restart, `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/` shows `work--0--0.txt` style files
2. Close a pane → after the next 30 s cycle, its `.txt` file is gone
3. `tsh cargo build` → rg finds the file, fzf preview shows matching lines, selecting jumps to session/window
4. Deep scrollback: run a long command, scroll far back, confirm `tsh <text>` still finds it


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/shunkakinoki/.claude/projects/-Users-shunkakinoki-dotfiles/f693495f-6f55-4063-a032-383f7c670a28.jsonl

If this plan can be broken down into multiple independent tasks, consider using the TeamCreate tool to create a team and parallelize the work.

# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
set-hook -g 'server-startup' 'run-shell -b "~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh"'

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes

  # With argument(s): search stored pane content files
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    if not test -d "$pane_dir"
      echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
      return
    end

    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    if test -z "$selected"
      return
    end

    # First line of each pane file: "# session=<name> window=<idx> pane=<id>"
    set -l meta (head -n 1 "$selected")
    set -l sess (string match -rg 'session=(\S+)' $meta)
    set -l widx (string match -rg 'window=(\d+)' $meta)

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done

        if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
          tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"

                tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null
              } > "$PANE_DIR/$num.txt"
            done
      done
    '';
  };

#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes

  # With argument(s): search stored pane content files
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    if not test -d "$pane_dir"
      echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
      return
    end

    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    if test -z "$selected"
      return
    end

    # work--0--0.txt → sess=work, widx=0
    set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace '.txt' '')
    set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
    set -l sess $parts[1]
    set -l widx $parts[2]

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

content.text:
Here's the full picture of what's stored and where:

---

## Directory layout

```
~/.local/share/tmux/
├── session-history.log        # window metadata (one line per window per 30s cycle)
└── panes/
    ├── work--0--0.txt         # session=work, window=0, pane=0
    ├── work--0--1.txt         # session=work, window=0, pane=1 (split)
    ├── work--1--0.txt         # session=work, window=1, pane=0
    ├── dotfiles--0--0.txt     # session=dotfiles, window=0, pane=0
    └── ...
```

**Filename format:** `<session_name>--<window_index>--<pane_index>.txt`

---

## Pane file contents

Each `.txt` file is the **raw terminal scrollback** of that pane — exactly what `tmux capture-pane -S -` produces. That means:

- All prior command output, going back as far as the scrollback buffer holds (now unlimited with `history-limit 0`)
- No metadata header — just the raw text
- Overwritten every 30 s cycle (rotate-to-`.old` → recapture → delete `.old`)
- If a pane is closed, its file disappears after the next cycle (the `.old` is never replaced, so it gets `rm`'d)

Example content of `work--0--0.txt`:
```
$ cargo build
   Compiling foo v0.1.0
   Finished dev [unoptimized] in 2.3s
$ git status
On branch main
nothing to commit
```

---

## Session history log

`session-history.log` appends one line **per open window** every 30 s:

```
2026-02-26T10:00:30  work:0  cargo  /Users/shunkakinoki/projects/foo
2026-02-26T10:00:30  work:1  vim    /Users/shunkakinoki/projects/foo
2026-02-26T10:01:00  work:0  cargo  /Users/shunkakinoki/projects/foo
```

Format: `<timestamp>  <session>:<window_index>  <window_name>  <cwd>`

No trim — grows forever (as you wanted).

---

## How `tsh` uses it

- **`tsh <query>`** — `rg` searches all `.txt` files for the query, fzf lets you pick the file, the filename is parsed (`work--1--0.txt` → sess=`work`, widx=`1`), then tmux switches you to that session/window
- **`tsh` (no args)** — fzf over the history log lines, pick a timestamp entry, same switch

Does this match what you expected, or do you want to change anything about the format?
This was an informational question, not a planning task — no implementation changes needed. No plan approval required here.

content.text:
Good catch — this is worth clarifying carefully. Let me think through exactly what happens:

---

## What "overwrite" actually means here

`tmux capture-pane -S -` captures **the entire scrollback buffer from the very beginning of the pane's life**, not just the last 30 seconds. With `history-limit 0`, the buffer grows forever and is never truncated.

So each cycle's overwrite looks like:

```
cycle 1 (t=30s):  file = buffer from pane birth → t=30s    (say 500 lines)
cycle 2 (t=60s):  file = buffer from pane birth → t=60s    (now 800 lines)
cycle 3 (t=90s):  file = buffer from pane birth → t=90s    (now 1100 lines)
```

Each new write **supersedes** the old one — it always contains everything the old one had, plus more. No history is lost between cycles.

---

## What IS actually lost

Two real gaps:

1. **Pane closes mid-cycle** — the last ≤30 s of output before a pane closes never gets captured. After the next cycle the `.old` file is deleted.

2. **tmux server restart** — pane scrollback buffers live in the tmux server process. When it dies, all buffers are gone regardless of what's on disk.

For both of these the only fix is `pipe-pane` (real-time streaming to a file), which was excluded from the plan due to complexity (ANSI stripping, log rotation, hook wiring).

---

**Do you want to revisit `pipe-pane` to close those gaps, or is the 30s snapshot cadence + unlimited buffer acceptable?**

questions.question: How do you want to handle the two real history gaps (last ≤30s before pane close, and tmux server restart)?

options.label: 30s snapshot is fine (Recommended)
options.description: Current approach: unlimited buffer + 30s capture cycle. Loses last ≤30s on pane close and loses everything on server restart. Simple, no extra complexity.

options.label: Add pipe-pane streaming
options.description: Real-time output streamed to per-pane log files. Captures everything including the gap. Requires ANSI stripping, log rotation, and hook wiring — significantly more complex.

The user wants:
1. Keep history for closed panes
2. Don't accumulate everything in a single file (split it up)
3. Make it ergonomic

So the idea would be:
- Keep the last snapshot of closed panes as an archive, but split them by session/date or something
- Not have one huge accumulation file
- Make it easy to search

Let me think about what a good approach would be:

**Option: Archive closed panes with timestamps**

Instead of deleting `.old` files, rename them to include a timestamp so they don't overwrite future captures:

```
panes/
  work--0--0.txt          # current live snapshot


```

I'm creating timestamped archive files for closed panes...

**Option: Per-session archive files**

When a pane closes, append its last content to a per-session archive file instead. This groups all closed panes from the same session together, keeping things organized without creating too many individual files.

**Option: Per-day archive files**

Organize archives by date, with each day getting its own log file.

**Option: Per-pane archive files with date in name**

Rename closed pane files to include the closure date rather than deleting them.

Now I'm thinking about what makes this ergonomic for the search workflow. Since `tsh <query>` uses `rg -l` to find matching files and then fzf to select, having multiple files is actually beneficial—the filename itself conveys the session, window, and pane information, and `rg -l` returns all matching paths at once for easy browsing.

So per-pane archive files with timestamps make the most sense. I'd keep closed panes in an `archive/` subdirectory with a timestamp suffix, then rotate live pane snapshots each cycle: move current `.txt` files to `.old`, capture fresh snapshots into new `.txt` files, and for any `.old` files without a corresponding live `.txt`, move them to the archive with a timestamp appended.

This keeps live panes in a single `panes/` directory that gets overwritten each cycle, preserves the last snapshot of closed panes in the archive, and lets the search command look across both locations without needing a monolithic blob file. # Plan: Pane Content Storage — Best Approach

## Context

The current pane snapshot approach has three problems:

1. **Opaque filenames** — files named `3.txt`, `7.txt` by numeric pane ID, which resets on server restart and is unreadable
2. **No stale cleanup** — files for closed panes accumulate forever; old IDs from previous server sessions leave ghost files
3. **Shallow scrollback** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` is bounded by `history-limit`, which defaults to only 2 000 lines

The fix is straightforward: readable `session--window--pane.txt` naming, atomic stale-file cleanup each cycle, and a large `history-limit` so the scrollback is deep enough to be genuinely useful.

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

---

## Changes

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`

Add after the existing session/window options:

```
set -g history-limit 50000
```

---

### 2. Standalone script + Nix reference

**Create:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done
```

**Update:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` — replace the inline `text = ''...''` block with a file source:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  source = ./session-logger.sh;
};
```

Key changes vs current:
- **Standalone file** — no Nix string escaping, easier to edit/test directly
- **Readable names** — `work--0--0.txt` instead of `3.txt`
- **Stale cleanup** — rotate-to-`.old`, recreate live, delete `.old`

---

### 3. Update `_tsh_function` to parse filename

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

Replace the metadata-header parsing with filename parsing:

```fish
# work--0--0.txt → sess=work, widx=0
set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace '.txt' '')
set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
set -l sess $parts[1]
set -l widx $parts[2]
```

Remove the `head -n 1` / `string match -rg 'session=...'` lines.

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `set -g history-limit 50000` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh` | **create** — standalone script |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Replace inline `text=` with `source = ./session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | Parse session/window from filename, drop header parsing |

---

## Verification

1. After server restart, `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/` shows `work--0--0.txt` style files
2. Close a pane → after the next 30 s cycle, its `.txt` file is gone
3. `tsh cargo build` → rg finds the file, fzf preview shows matching lines, selecting jumps to session/window
4. Deep scrollback: run a long command, scroll far back, confirm `tsh <text>` still finds it

# Plan: Pane Archive — Keep Closed Pane History

## Context

The current implementation already has:
- Readable `session--window--pane.txt` filenames
- `history-limit 0` (unlimited scrollback)
- 30s capture cycle via `session-logger.sh`
- `_tsh_function.fish` parsing filenames to jump to sessions

The remaining gap: when a pane closes, the next cycle rotates its `.txt` to `.old` and then **deletes it**, losing the last known state forever. The user wants closed-pane snapshots preserved for later searching — but split into per-pane files (not a single blob), and kept ergonomic.

---

## Changes

### 1. Update `session-logger.sh` — archive closed panes instead of deleting

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Change the rotation logic:

- After recapturing all live panes, check each `.old` file
- If a matching `.txt` exists → pane still alive → delete the `.old` (unchanged behaviour)
- If **no** matching `.txt` exists → pane closed → move to `archive/` with a timestamp suffix

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
ARCHIVE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/archive

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Rotate existing live snapshots to .old
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  # Recapture all currently live panes
  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done
done
```

---

### 2. Update `_tsh_function.fish` — search archive too

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

- Add `archive_dir` variable pointing at `~/.local/share/tmux/archive`
- Pass both `$pane_dir` and `$archive_dir` to `rg -l` so search covers live + closed panes
- Parse filenames from archive: `work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt` → sess=`work`, widx=`0`
  - Strip the trailing `--<timestamp>` before splitting on `--`
  - Since archived panes no longer exist, skip the `tmux has-session` jump and instead just print the file path / preview content

The archive filename format `sess--widx--pidx--YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.txt` lets us extract `parts[1]` and `parts[2]` the same way as live files. For dead sessions, we print a message but still show the content.

```fish
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
  set -l archive_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/archive

  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    if not test -d "$pane_dir"
      echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
      return
    end

    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" "$archive_dir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    if test -z "$selected"
      return
    end

    # work--0--0.txt or work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt → sess=work, widx=0
    set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' | string replace '.txt' '')
    set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
    set -l sess $parts[1]
    set -l widx $parts[2]

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists (archived pane — content shown above in preview)"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

---

## Final directory layout

```
~/.local/share/tmux/
├── session-history.log              # window metadata log (grows unbounded)
├── panes/
│   ├── work--0--0.txt               # live pane snapshots (overwritten each cycle)
│   └── dotfiles--0--0.txt
└── archive/
    ├── work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt   # closed pane, last snapshot before close
    └── work--1--0--20260226-091500.txt
```

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh` | Replace `rm -f *.old` with archive-or-delete logic |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | Search `archive/` too; handle archive filename format; graceful message for dead sessions |

---

## Verification

1. Open a pane, wait 30s → `panes/work--0--0.txt` exists
2. Close that pane, wait 30s → `panes/work--0--0.txt` gone, `archive/work--0--0--<ts>.txt` exists
3. `tsh <text that was in the closed pane>` → fzf shows the archive file, preview shows matching lines
4. Selecting an archived pane prints "Session no longer exists" rather than erroring
5. `tsh <text in live pane>` → still navigates to the live session/window as before

# Plan: Pane Content Storage — Best Approach

## Context

The current pane snapshot approach has three problems:

1. **Opaque filenames** — files named `3.txt`, `7.txt` by numeric pane ID, which resets on server restart and is unreadable
2. **No stale cleanup** — files for closed panes accumulate forever; old IDs from previous server sessions leave ghost files
3. **Shallow scrollback** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` is bounded by `history-limit`, which defaults to only 2 000 lines

The fix is straightforward: readable `session--window--pane.txt` naming, atomic stale-file cleanup each cycle, and a large `history-limit` so the scrollback is deep enough to be genuinely useful.

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

---

## Changes

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`

Add after the existing session/window options:

```
set -g history-limit 50000
```

---

### 2. Standalone script + Nix reference

**Create:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done
```

**Update:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` — replace the inline `text = ''...''` block with a file source:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  source = ./session-logger.sh;
};
```

Key changes vs current:
- **Standalone file** — no Nix string escaping, easier to edit/test directly
- **Readable names** — `work--0--0.txt` instead of `3.txt`
- **Stale cleanup** — rotate-to-`.old`, recreate live, delete `.old`

---

### 3. Update `_tsh_function` to parse filename

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

Replace the metadata-header parsing with filename parsing:

```fish
# work--0--0.txt → sess=work, widx=0
set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace '.txt' '')
set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
set -l sess $parts[1]
set -l widx $parts[2]
```

Remove the `head -n 1` / `string match -rg 'session=...'` lines.

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `set -g history-limit 50000` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh` | **create** — standalone script |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Replace inline `text=` with `source = ./session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | Parse session/window from filename, drop header parsing |

---

## Verification

1. After server restart, `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/` shows `work--0--0.txt` style files
2. Close a pane → after the next 30 s cycle, its `.txt` file is gone
3. `tsh cargo build` → rg finds the file, fzf preview shows matching lines, selecting jumps to session/window
4. Deep scrollback: run a long command, scroll far back, confirm `tsh <text>` still finds it

# Plan: Pane Archive — Keep Closed Pane History

## Context

The current implementation already has:
- Readable `session--window--pane.txt` filenames
- `history-limit 0` (unlimited scrollback)
- 30s capture cycle via `session-logger.sh`
- `_tsh_function.fish` parsing filenames to jump to sessions

The remaining gap: when a pane closes, the next cycle rotates its `.txt` to `.old` and then **deletes it**, losing the last known state forever. The user wants closed-pane snapshots preserved for later searching — but split into per-pane files (not a single blob), and kept ergonomic.

---

## Changes

### 1. Update `session-logger.sh` — archive closed panes instead of deleting

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Change the rotation logic:

- After recapturing all live panes, check each `.old` file
- If a matching `.txt` exists → pane still alive → delete the `.old` (unchanged behaviour)
- If **no** matching `.txt` exists → pane closed → move to `archive/` with a timestamp suffix

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
ARCHIVE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/archive

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Rotate existing live snapshots to .old
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  # Recapture all currently live panes
  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done
done
```

---

**`session-logger.sh`** — the old `rm -f *.old` is replaced with a check per file:
- `.old` with a matching live `.txt` → pane still open → delete `.old`
- `.old` with **no** matching `.txt` → pane closed → move to `archive/` as `work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt`

**`_tsh_function.fish`** — `rg` now searches both `panes/` and `archive/`; the filename parser strips the trailing `--YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` before splitting, so archive files parse identically to live ones; selecting an archived pane that no longer exists prints a graceful message instead of erroring.

default.nix
tmuxinator
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if test "$selected" = work
      set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
      set -l restore $restore[1]
      if test -n "$restore"
        tmux run-shell "$restore"
      end
      if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
        if test -n "$TMUX"
          tmux switch-client -t work
        else
          tmux attach-session -t work
        end
      else
        tmuxinator start work
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
function _tsw_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
function _two_function --description "Attach to tmux work session"
  set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
  set -l restore $restore[1]
  if test -n "$restore"
    tmux run-shell "$restore"
  end

  if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t work
    else
      tmux attach-session -t work
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start work
  end
end
